refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combines multiple cycle phases (load, travel, dump) into one requirement, which implies multiple obligations. To be atomic and solution-neutral, it is necessary to separate the cycle time targets for each phase to clearly define individual functional requirements without implying how to achieve them.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load cycle sequencing and timing
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load cycle with modular task parallelism
Incorporate buffer and staging areas to smooth load cycle variability
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback

potential_sources: Dependency on accurate real-time data acquisition
Integration with safety and operational policy modules
coupling_check.notes: Adaptive control logically decouples cycle time achievement from fixed scheduling by using feedback, supporting independence. Potential coupling arises if feedback data or control actions are tightly integrated with other system functions, but this can be managed with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Real-time feedback data is reliable and timely
Control algorithms can respond within cycle time constraints
Safety and operational policies do not impose rigid cycle time limits that conflict with adaptive control
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including interface requirements for feedback data acquisition and control actuation to ensure modularity and independence in the DP design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for overall system control and adaptive cycle time management

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time feedback data for adaptive control

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated cycle time commands to execution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive cycle time control is a core system control function; minimal diffusion as it mainly interacts via telemetry and tasking interfaces.
Vehicle module is more about physical actuation; adaptive control logic would be diffused across vehicle subsystems.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; adaptive cycle time control is less relevant and would cause diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring; adaptive cycle time control is outside its scope, causing high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing adaptive cycle time control because it centralizes system control logic and can effectively manage real-time feedback and cycle time adjustments with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency or failures.
Risk of coupling with Tasking interface leading to tight integration that could complicate module independence.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback' directly addresses the FR of supporting the customer-defined cycle time target by adapting cycle time dynamically, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of achieving p95 ≤ CT_target under specified conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency or failures.
Risk of coupling with Tasking interface leading to tight integration that could complicate module independence.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion, centralizing the adaptive cycle time control logic effectively. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, indicating that the chosen IM boundary is appropriate to minimize diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the measured cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, leveraging telemetry data and system logs from the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the adaptive cycle time control function to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces for telemetry input and tasking output supporting the implementation.

recommended_revisions: Consider implementing fallback or buffering strategies to mitigate risks from telemetry data latency or failures.
Design the Tasking interface to minimize tight coupling and preserve module independence, possibly through well-defined abstraction layers or messaging protocols.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear alignment, feasible verification, and appropriate module allocation. The adaptive cycle time control function is well-defined and allocated to a high-cohesion module with manageable diffusion risks. Although there are identified risks related to telemetry data latency and module coupling, these are noted as recommended revisions rather than blockers for further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize this level with mitigation strategies rather than further decomposing, which could introduce complexity and verification challenges.

revise.why: To implement fallback or buffering strategies to mitigate telemetry data latency/failure risks and to design the Tasking interface to minimize tight coupling and preserve module independence.

assumptions: Telemetry data will be sufficiently reliable with fallback mechanisms.
The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate the adaptive control logic effectively.
open_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data affected by network latency or failures.
Risk of tight coupling with Tasking interface complicating module independence.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing p95 cycle time meets CT_target under specified conditions.
Demonstration of fallback strategies effectiveness in telemetry data loss scenarios.
Validation of Tasking interface abstraction minimizing coupling.
